title: datalink tooling per-repo config
description: Per-repo parameters that drive the shared, DB-agnostic catalog tooling engine in datalink/tooling. Each consuming repo (sqlink, ducklink) ships one of these (as tooling/datalink.config.json or passed via --config) plus its own templates/, compat-registry.json data, lessons-learned.md, and registry index. The engine scripts (scaffold, smoke, t-status, compat, registry) read this file to resolve every DB-specific bit. Paths are relative to repo_root unless absolute.

db_name.description: The target engine: 'duckdb' or 'sqlite'. Drives default messages and is the discriminator for behaviour the other fields don't fully capture.

registration_abi.description: How an extension registers with the host. 'manifest' = a static describe() manifest (sqlite-wasm). 'imperative' = registers in load() against the host capability registry (ducklink). Selects scaffold template semantics and is recorded for downstream tooling.

repo_root.description: Repo root. Defaults to the parent of the directory containing the config file when omitted; when the config lives in <repo>/tooling/ this resolves to <repo>. May be absolute or relative to the config file.

wit.description: WIT package / world coordinates and the per-extension WIT source to copy. Pure-manifest repos (sqlite-wasm) may omit this entirely.

source_dir.description: Directory whose wit/ tree is copied into each scaffolded extension (relative to repo_root). Omit if extensions do not carry a per-crate WIT copy.

extensions_dir.description: Directory (relative to repo_root) under which extension crates live, e.g. 'extensions'.

package_suffix.description: Appended to the bare name to form the crate/dir name. sqlink: '' (dir == name). ducklink: '-component'.

name_pattern.description: Regex the bare extension name must match. sqlink is lowercase-only; ducklink allows uppercase.

templates_dir.description: Directory (relative to repo_root) holding the *.tmpl files. Each repo keeps its own templates/.

worlds.description: Map of world-name -> [lib.rs template filename, one-line focus hint]. Drives scaffold's --world flag. A single-world repo uses one entry (e.g. {'default': ['lib.rs.tmpl', 'scalar functions']}); the default world is the first key or 'minimal'/'default' if present.

lib_template_vars.description: Which template variables the selected lib.rs template expects. The engine always supplies NAME, NAME_UNDERSCORE, DESCRIPTION_SHORT; list the subset actually used so the renderer can pass exactly those (sqlite minimal uses NAME+DESCRIPTION_SHORT; ducklink uses NAME+NAME_UNDERSCORE+DESCRIPTION_SHORT).

register_workspace_member.description: If true, insert 'extensions/<pkg>' into the root Cargo.toml [workspace].members array (ducklink). If false, the scaffolded Cargo.toml carries its own inline [workspace] and no root edit happens (sqlink).

build_check.description: The post-scaffold cargo build-check used to confirm the skeleton compiles.

argv.description: Argv template. Tokens {PKG}, {NAME}, {TARGET} are substituted. e.g. sqlink ['cargo','check','--release','--target','{TARGET}'] run in the crate dir; ducklink ['cargo','check','-p','{PKG}','--target','{TARGET}','--release'] run in repo_root.

cwd.description: Where to run the build-check: in the new crate dir ('crate', sqlink) or at repo_root ('repo_root', ducklink).

shared_target_dir.description: If set, CARGO_TARGET_DIR is pointed here (relative to repo_root) so common deps don't recompile per scaffold (sqlink uses extensions/_shared-target).

argv.description: Argv template for the smoke CLI invocation. Tokens substituted: {HOST_BIN}, {CLI_COMPONENT}, {EXTENSIONS_DIR}, {NAME} (bare), {DB}. The SQL is piped on stdin. sqlink: ['{HOST_BIN}','{CLI_COMPONENT}','--db','{DB}']. ducklink: ['{HOST_BIN}','--extensions-dir','{EXTENSIONS_DIR}','--','{DB}','--load-extension','{NAME}'].

host_bin.description: Path (relative to repo_root) to the host runner binary for {HOST_BIN}. sqlink target/release/sqlink; ducklink target/release/ducklink.

cli_component.description: Path (relative to repo_root) to the CLI wasm component for {CLI_COMPONENT}, when the argv uses it (sqlink).

required_artifacts.description: Extra files (relative to repo_root) that must exist before a smoke can run; reported as a friendly 'not built' message otherwise. e.g. ducklink core+cli components.

extensions_dir.description: Directory (relative to repo_root) the host resolves <name>.wasm artifacts from, for {EXTENSIONS_DIR} (ducklink artifacts/extensions). Also where --build copies built artifacts.

package_suffix.description: Same suffix as scaffold.package_suffix; used to map a bare name to its extensions/<name><suffix>/ directory holding smoke.sql / smoke.expected.

prompt_pattern.description: Regex (anchored at line start) stripping the REPL prompts from output. sqlink '^(sqlite>\s*|\s*\.\.\.>\s*)+'; ducklink '^(D>\s*|\.\.\.>\s*)+'.

sql_preamble.description: Prepended to the smoke SQL before piping. sqlink '.nullvalue <NULL>\n' (so NULL renders literally); ducklink '.mode csv\n'.

null_token.description: The literal a NULL renders as in parsed output. Used by the all-NULL heuristic. sqlink '<NULL>'; ducklink 'NULL'.

banner_prefixes.description: Output lines starting with any of these are dropped during parsing (load banners). sqlink ['Loaded extension:'].

panic_markers.description: Substrings in combined stdout+stderr that signal a load/panic failure.

comment_prefix.description: Line-comment prefix stripped from smoke.sql before piping (so multi-line comments don't fuse onto the next statement). SQL uses '--'.

env.description: Extra environment for the smoke subprocess. Values may contain {NAME}. ducklink {'DUCKLINK_NETWORK_GRANT': '{NAME}'}.

supports_build.description: If true, --build is available: cargo component build the crate and copy its artifact into extensions_dir before smoking (ducklink). sqlink loads the per-crate component directly via .load in smoke.sql and has no separate build step.

build_argv.description: Argv template for --build (when supports_build). Tokens {PKG}, {TARGET}. ducklink ['cargo','component','build','-p','{PKG}','--target','{TARGET}','--release'].

build_output.description: Built wasm path (relative to repo_root) produced by build_argv, tokens {UNDERSCORE},{TARGET_DIR}. Copied to <extensions_dir>/<name>.wasm. ducklink target/wasm32-wasip2/release/{UNDERSCORE}_component.wasm.

build_target.description: The rustc target triple used for {TARGET} in build_argv and for locating the build output's target/<triple>/release dir.

all_null_min_rows.description: Minimum parsed rows before the all-NULL heuristic fires.

feedback.description: Parameters for t-status.py (the lessons-learned / T-N feedback system). The format is shared (see spec/lessons-learned.spec.md); only the doc path is per-repo.

lessons_doc.description: Path (relative to repo_root) to the lessons-learned.md scanned for (T-N new)/(T-N closed) markers.

compat_registry.description: Parameters for compat.py / scaffold's crate-compat lookups.

path.description: Path (relative to repo_root) to the per-repo compat-registry.json. The _schema + reader are shared; the crate data is per-repo.

registry.description: Parameters for registry.py (the catalog index reader/validator for COMMON fields only; identity/digest verification is the DEFERRED identity module, not implemented here).

index_path.description: Path (relative to repo_root) to the catalog index. sqlink registry/index.json (entries under 'extensions'); ducklink registry/index.json. NOTE: ducklink's index is owned by the Phase 1 identity work; registry.py reads it read-only for common-field validation only.

entries_key.description: Top-level key holding the array of entries, or '' if the index is itself a list/map of entries.

identity.description: Parameters for the content-addressed identity engine (identity.py + gen.py + verify.py). Drives the TWO digest schemes lifted from ducklink's Phase 1 (both reimplemented byte-identically from compose-core::blobs): the witcanon CONTRACT digest (sha256("witcanon:1" || canonical WIT bytes), always-enforced shape identity) and the content BYTE digest (sha256 of each .wasm, opt-in-enforced). All paths are relative to repo_root unless absolute.

wit_source_dir.description: The CANONICAL contract WIT directory whose top-level *.wit files (sorted by filename, concatenated) form the witcanon digest. This is the authoritative contract package dir, NOT necessarily the per-crate propagated copy in wit.source_dir. ducklink: 'wit/duckdb-extension' (16 files; deps.toml excluded by the *.wit glob). sqlink: 'wit'. If omitted, falls back to wit.source_dir.

contract_package.description: The WIT package id imported by a built artifact, parsed from `wasm-tools component wit` for the @MAJOR cross-check. ducklink 'duckdb:extension'; sqlink 'sqlink:wasm'. If omitted, falls back to wit.package.

contract_major.description: The contract MAJOR the catalog targets, matched against both wit_contract_version and the built artifact's imported <package>@MAJOR. ducklink '2'; sqlink '0' (sqlink:wasm@0.1.0). If omitted, derived from contract_version.

contract_version.description: The human-readable contract version stamped into each entry's wit_contract_version (the runtime-observable proxy). ducklink '2.0.0'; sqlink '0.1.0'.

artifacts_dir.description: Directory holding the deployed <name>.wasm artifacts whose content_digest is computed/verified. ducklink 'artifacts/extensions'. If omitted, falls back to smoke.extensions_dir.

wasm_tools_bin.description: The `wasm-tools` binary used for the imported-@MAJOR cross-check in verify.py.

exclude.description: Entry names excluded from verify.py's identity checks (parity with ducklink's verify-catalog, which filters `sample_extension` -- the template extension importing the legacy unversioned contract by design -- out of its digest loop). ducklink ['sample_extension'].

type_map.description: PLACEHOLDER for the Tier-2 ValueModel/TypeMap difference (DuckDB rich logical types vs SQLite five storage classes). The current tooling does not consume this beyond recording it; it marks where deeper (host-engine) tooling will branch. Left as a free-form pass-through.
